stackRefinements.ui: React 18 + TypeScript, built with Vite (manifest confirms; tests via node --test)
stackRefinements.api: Express 4 + TypeScript (manifest confirms; tests via node --test)
stackRefinements.shared: TypeScript shared package defined by Zod schemas (validation/types provider)

ordering.summary: shared is the only provider — consumed at runtime by both ui and api — so in cross-layer tasks shared changes first, then api and ui (no edge between them; either order). db has no internal edges in either direction and can be sequenced independently.

gaps: db has zero internal consumers: api neither declares db in its manifest nor imports it in source (apps/api/src/server.ts imports only express). Either the api→db wiring is missing/undeclared or the db layer is currently dead weight — confirm with the team before deriving dispatch rules that assume api depends on db.
Internal dependency on shared uses bare version "*" rather than the workspace:* protocol; resolution relies on npm workspaces hoisting. Works, but worth noting as a convention signal.
